kept.q: In the year 2021, who among the following became the first Indian Chairperson of the United Nations Advisory Committee of the Human Rights Council (HRC)?
Identify the FALSE statement in the context of digestion.
o: Assimilation and absorption both occur before egestion.
Digestion comes before egestion but after ingestion.
Absorption comes before assimilation but after ingestion.
Assimilation comes before ingestion but after absorption.

Ron is sitting second to the right of Tyler. Sarah is sitting third to the left of Adam. Laura is sitting to the immediate left of Tyler. Tessa is sitting to the immediate right of Ron. Who is sitting to the immediate left of Sarah?

A sum of ₹5,000 is divided into two parts. One part is deposited in bank A at simple interest at 5% per annum, and the other part is deposited in bank B at simple interest at 10% per annum. If the total annual interest received is ₹325, then the money deposited in bank A was:

Refer to the following letter series and answer the question that follows.
P L M O K I J N B H U Y G A V E C F T
How many such consonants are there in the above series, each of which is immediately preceded by a vowel and immediately followed by a vowel?

In this question, a statement is followed by two conclusions numbered I and II. You have to assume everything in the statement to be true and on the basis of which of them logically follows beyond a reasonable doubt from the information given in the statement.
Statement:
Providing LPG connections to BPL (Below Poverty Level) households will ensure universal coverage of cooking gas in country X.
Conclusions:
I. Usage of inefficient cooking fuels such as firewood, coal, etc., is a common practice in rural parts of country X.
II. No other mode of cooking is as effective as using LPG.
o: Neither conclusion I nor II follows
Both conclusions I and II follow

kept.q: Suppose an object of height 5 cm is placed in front of a concave mirror and a real and inverted image of the same size is formed at a distance of 20.0 cm. Then the centre of curvature of the mirror is at:

U, V, W, X, Y and Z are six students sitting around a circular table, facing the centre of the table for a school assignment. X is the immediate neighbour of W. V is seated third to the left of W. U is seated to the immediate right of Y. V is seated second to the right of X. Who is seated third to the left of Z?

z(x³ − y³)/(x² + xy + y²) + x(y³ − z³)/(y² + yz + z²) + y(z³ − x³)/(z² + zx + x²)

What was the main reason behind Mendel's success in deriving the laws of inheritance?
o: He studied only one character at a time in his crosses.
He chose parents that were identical.
He counted the individuals exhibiting a particular trait in each generation.
